2606.30498.title: On the Faithfulness of Post-Hoc Concept Bottleneck Models
2606.30498.paper_summary: Human decision-making interprets the world through high-level concepts, such as recognizing a bird by its belly color. To bridge the gap between opaque deep learning representations and human understanding, Post-Hoc Concept Bottleneck Models (post-hoc CBMs) project latent features onto interpretable concept spaces using auxiliary datasets or vision-language models. However, relying on target task accuracy as the primary measure of post-hoc CBM success obscures whether the learned concepts are semantically meaningful or merely predictive artifacts. For example, random concept projections can achieve competitive accuracy despite being semantically meaningless. In this work, we analyze the learned projections directly and identify two failure cases: First, for concept projections learned from auxiliary data, covariate shifts can lead to unfaithful concept representations for the target task. In particular, we provide an upper bound on the error introduced by this shift. Second, systematic label noise in surrogate concept labels generated by vision-language models leads to unfaithful projections. After formalizing these failure modes, we introduce novel metrics that decouple concept faithfulness from predictive accuracy. Our empirical results across real-world and synthetic benchmarks confirm that these metrics identify unfaithful behaviors that standard accuracy-based evaluation fails to detect.
2606.30498.paper_summary_zh: 人類的決策過程透過高階概念來解讀世界，例如根據鳥類的腹部顏色來識別一隻鳥。為了縮小不透明的深度學習表徵與人類理解之間的差距，事後概念瓶頸模型（post-hoc CBMs）利用輔助數據集或視覺-語言模型將潛在特徵投影到可解釋的概念空間。然而，將目標任務的準確性作為事後 CBM 成功的主要衡量標準，會掩蓋所學概念是否具有語義意義或僅僅是預測性工件。例如，隨機概念投影可以在語義上毫無意義的情況下達到競爭性的準確性。在這項工作中，我們直接分析所學的投影並確定兩種失敗情況：首先，從輔助數據學習的概念投影中，協變移動可能導致目標任務的概念表徵不忠實。特別是，我們提供了這種移動所引入的誤差的上限。其次，由視覺-語言模型生成的替代概念標籤中的系統性標籤噪聲導致不忠實的投影。在正式化這些失敗模式之後，我們引入了新穎的度量標準，將概念的忠實性與預測準確性解耦。我們在現實世界和合成基準上的實證結果確認這些度量標準能夠識別標準基於準確性的評估未能檢測到的不忠實行為。
2606.30498.author: Laines Schmalwasser et.al.
2606.30498.authors: Laines Schmalwasser, Jan Blunk, Niklas Penzel, Julia Niebling, Joachim Denzler
